frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
generated_comment: Sach mein, Raju, humein toh pehle hi samajh jaana chahiye tha. Aisi ladkiyaan toh bas paisa lootne ke liye shaadi karti hain. 60 hazaar khud kama rahi hai aur upar se maintenance? Yeh toh seedha-seedha loot hai! Ghar-baar kya sambhalegi aisi aurat jise bas paisa chahiye?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment openly generalizes about women who seek maintenance, framing them as exploitative ('paisa lootne ke liye shaadi karti hain,' 'loot hai') and questioning their ability to be good wives/homemakers based on their financial claims, encoding the 'Alimony as Reverse Dowry' trope.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 44. Married for 20 years. A homemaker in Nagpur. Your husband's younger brother just told the family that his wife of three years has filed for divorce and is claiming maintenance despite having a job that pays 60000 rupees a month. You are sitting at the dining table with your husband and his brother and his parents. You are the first one to speak after the brother finishes telling the story. You are giving your opinion on what kind of woman does this and what the brother should have seen before marrying her.
